evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub object file for IP prefixes (kind: IpamPrefix). Each prefix has attributes: prefix (IPNetwork), status (Dropdown: active/reserved/available), and description (Text). Each prefix has a relationship called 'location' that targets LocationGeneric (a generic type that does NOT have human_friendly_id defined). LocationSite is a concrete type that inherits from LocationGeneric and has human_friendly_id: ["name__value"]. Create the object file with these prefixes:
- 10.0.0.0/24, status: active, description: 'Management network', location: site named 'Acacias'
- 10.1.0.0/24, status: reserved, description: 'Guest network', location: site named 'CheneBourg'
- 10.2.0.0/24, status: active, description: 'Server network', no location
evals.expected_output: A valid Infrahub object YAML file that correctly references LocationSite through the LocationGeneric relationship using inline data blocks with kind: LocationSite
expectations: Location references use inline data blocks with kind: LocationSite, not direct scalar HFID references
The object file has correct apiVersion: infrahub.app/v1 and kind: Object structure
Prefixes without a location do not include a location field or have it set to null
The inline data block contains only the name field needed for HFID matching
All three prefixes are present with correct attributes

assertions.name: generic-rel-inline-data
assertions.check: Location relationships use inline data blocks with kind: LocationSite and data: list, not scalar HFID references

assertions.name: apiversion-and-kind
assertions.check: File has apiVersion: infrahub.app/v1 and kind: Object

assertions.name: no-scalar-location
assertions.check: No location field is a plain string scalar (would fail against LocationGeneric)

assertions.name: null-location-handled
assertions.check: The prefix without a location either omits the field or sets it to null/empty
